mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and rising recession probability, causing S&P 500 forward returns to turn negative over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 65 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent, or the signal loses predictive power during non-recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real savings alongside falling federal funds rates signal abundant liquidity seeking yield, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and FEDFUNDS move in opposite directions, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio drops below 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals strengthening corporate output and capacity utilization, which translates to positive earnings revisions and immediate upward pressure on S&P 500 prices over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during periods of supply chain bottlenecks or when inventory accumulation exceeds sales growth.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent confirms robust economic expansion, reducing default risk and supporting corporate profit margins, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative in more than 55 percent of quarters where GDPC1 growth surpasses 2.0 percent, indicating a decoupling between macro growth and equity valuations.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in total nonfarm payrolls and housing starts indicate synchronized labor and construction demand, signaling broad-based economic strength that drives positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal loses predictive accuracy when housing starts rise due to speculative bubbles while payroll growth stagnates, resulting in negative forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels erode real corporate profit margins and trigger expectations of tighter monetary policy, which systematically reduces S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases fail to predict negative returns when wage growth outpaces inflation, or when the Federal Reserve explicitly commits to maintaining accommodative policy despite price rises.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payroll additions exceeding 150 thousand signal robust labor market expansion, boosting consumer spending expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain negative in over 60 percent of months where payroll growth exceeds 150 thousand, indicating that labor strength is being priced in or offset by rate hike fears.
